mechanism_narrative: Near-zero federal funds rates reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive forward S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20d forward returns are negative in >55% of observations when FEDFUNDS < 0.25%, or Rank IC < 0.02 over 3-year rolling windows.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate exceeds 9.0%, weak labor market conditions suppress consumer spending expectations and corporate earnings growth, driving negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 60d returns in >60% of triggered instances, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous declines in retail sales and housing starts signal a broad contraction in consumer demand, which directly reduces corporate revenue forecasts and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint decline in RSAFS and HOUST fails to predict negative returns, with a positive return frequency exceeding 50% in triggered months.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the index of industrial production signals strengthening manufacturing output and supply-side capacity utilization, which generates positive short-term S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: INDPRO growth fails to predict positive 5d returns during early-cycle phases, with a negative return frequency above 55% or Rank IC < 0.01.

mechanism_narrative: Rising inflation combined with stagnant policy rates erodes real yields and prompts expectations of future monetary tightening, which compresses equity valuations and yields negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The combination of rising CPIAUCSL and low FEDFUNDS produces positive 60d returns in >65% of cases, invalidating the tightening expectation channel.

mechanism_narrative: Month-over-month growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising wage income, which supports corporate earnings and drives positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows no predictive power for 20d returns, with a negative return frequency exceeding 50% or a t-statistic below 1.5 in out-of-sample tests.

mechanism_narrative: Quarterly GDP growth exceeding 2.0% confirms sustained aggregate demand expansion, which boosts equity risk appetite and generates positive S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 5 trading days following data release.
failure_condition: GDP growth above 2.0% fails to trigger positive 5d returns, with negative returns occurring in >60% of post-release windows.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence between rising industrial production and falling unemployment indicates improving labor productivity and supply-side efficiency, which enhances corporate profit margins and yields positive S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The INDPRO-UNRATE divergence produces negative 60d returns in >55% of triggered instances, or the interaction term loses significance in regression tests.
